metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1759
metadata.comment: Rationale: Latrophilin-2 loss in postsynaptic hippocampal neurons cultured from KO mice reduced spine density and staining for excitatory markers but not inhibitory synapses, as shown in Fig. 2B-J. In agreement, Fig. 2K,L shows that postsynaptic loss of latrophilin-2 in cultured hippocampal neurons reduces the frequency and amplitude of excitatory miniature events. Fig. 4 shows that latrophilin-2 is also required in vivo for normal spine numbers and its loss reduces spine numbers in CA1 stratum lacunosum moleculare, where it is expressed, but not in other strata. This region-specific effect on functional synapse assembly is supported by the result that postsynaptic Lphn2 deletion in CA1 impairs excitatory synaptic transmission of entorhinal cortex inputs, shown in Fig. 5.. Experimental description: Conditional Lphn2 KO mice were generated. Hippocampal neurons were cultured from Lphn2 cKO mice.
